name: ComponentInstance
summary: The ComponentInstance class is used to represent component instances of a component definition or components that have been dragged from the Component Browser and placed (thus, instanced) within the Model. Therefore, the ComponentInstance class contains a reference to a corresponding ComponentDefinition object and a Transformation object (which contains the location of the component in the Drawing Window). Starting from SketchUp 2018+, the ComponentInstance class contains default attributes when created or imported. The attributes are: “Owner”, “Status”. See the Help article for more information. The dictionary cannot be deleted via ruby and an @raise ArgumentError will be raised. The key/value pairs in the dictionary can be deleted safely.

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: equals?
methods.summary: The equals? method is used to determine if a component instance is geometrically equivalent to another instance.
methods.remarks: The equals? method is used to determine if a component instance is geometrically equivalent to another instance.

params.name: instance
params.doc: The instance to compare this instance with.

methods.name: explode
methods.summary: The explode method is used to explode the component instance into separate entities.
methods.remarks: The explode method is used to explode the component instance into separate entities.

returns.doc: An array of entity objects if successful, false if unsuccessful

methods.name: guid
methods.summary: The guid method is used to get the base 64 encoded unique id for this SketchUp object.
methods.remarks: The guid method is used to get the base 64 encoded unique id for this SketchUp object.

methods.name: intersect
methods.summary: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make.
methods.remarks: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make. The intersect method is used to compute the boolean intersection of two instances representing manifold solid volumes (this - arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: instance
params.doc: The instance to intersect this instance with.

returns.doc: The resultant group if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds otherwise nil is returned.

methods.name: locked?
methods.summary: The locked? method is used to determine if a component instance is locked.
methods.remarks: The locked? method is used to determine if a component instance is locked.

methods.name: make_unique
methods.summary: The #make_unique method is used to create a component definition for this instance that is not used by any other instances.
methods.remarks: The #make_unique method is used to create a component definition for this instance that is not used by any other instances. If the component is already unique in the model, nothing happens.

methods.name: manifold?
methods.summary: The manifold? method is used to determine if an instance is manifold.
methods.remarks: The manifold? method is used to determine if an instance is manifold.

methods.name: move!
methods.summary: Note: Despite the name being similar to #transform!, this method closer corresponds to #transformation=.
methods.remarks: Note: Despite the name being similar to #transform!, this method closer corresponds to #transformation=. The #move! method is used to set the transformation of this component instance, similarly to #transformation= but without recording to the undo stack. This method is useful for moving entities inside of an animation or page transition.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful

methods.name: outer_shell
methods.summary: The outer_shell method is used to compute the outer shell of the two instances representing manifold solid volumes (this || arg).
methods.remarks: The outer_shell method is used to compute the outer shell of the two instances representing manifold solid volumes (this || arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: instance
params.doc: The instance to outer shell this instance with.

returns.doc: The resultant group if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds otherwise nil is returned.

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: show_differences
methods.summary: The show_differences method is used to determine if a component instance is geometrically equivalent to another instance and in addition move the non- matching and matching geometry to new layers.
methods.remarks: The show_differences method is used to determine if a component instance is geometrically equivalent to another instance and in addition move the non- matching and matching geometry to new layers. This method will move both instances to Layer0. Geometry that is the same in both components will be moved to a new layer named def_name + “_same”. Geometry that is not the same will be moved to a layer named def_name + “_diff”. If verbose is true, a list of all the geometry that is different from one component to the other is displayed texturally in the Ruby Console.

params.name: instance
params.doc: The instance to be compared with.

params.name: verbose
params.doc: A boolean flag indicating whether to display a textural report of the found differences to the Ruby console.

returns.doc: true if the instances are geometrically equivalent, otherwise false.

methods.name: split
methods.summary: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make.
methods.remarks: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make. The split method is used to compute the boolean split (map overlay)of the two instances representing manifold solid volumes (this - arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails. resultant groups if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds otherwise nil is returned. The 3 groups are as follows: The intersection of volume 1 & volume 2, the difference of volume 1 minus volume 2, and the reverse difference of volume 2 minus volume 1.

params.name: instance
params.doc: The instance to split this instance with.

methods.name: subtract
methods.summary: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make.
methods.remarks: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make. The subtract method is used to compute the boolean difference of the two instances representing manifold solid volumes (this - arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: instance
params.doc: The instance to subtract this instance from.

returns.doc: The resultant group if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds otherwise nil is returned.

methods.name: transform!
methods.summary: Apply a Geom::Transformation to a component instance.
methods.remarks: Apply a Geom::Transformation to a component instance.

params.name: transform
params.doc: The transformation object to apply to the component instance.

methods.name: trim
methods.summary: Note: Trimming object instance2 using instance1 results in a new trimmed version of instance2.
methods.remarks: Note: Trimming object instance2 using instance1 results in a new trimmed version of instance2. If the trim is successful the original instance2 is erased and a newly trimmed version is created. This new version, derived from the trimming operation, will possess a new GUID and will be returned. Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make. The #trim method is used to compute the (non-destructive) boolean difference of the two instances representing manifold solid volumes (this - arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: instance
params.doc: The instance to trim this instance against.

returns.doc: The resultant group if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds otherwise nil is returned.

methods.name: union
methods.summary: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make.
methods.remarks: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make. The union method is used to compute the boolean union of the two instances representing manifold solid volumes (this | arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: instance
params.doc: The instance to union this instance with.

returns.doc: The resultant group if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds otherwise nil is returned.

methods.name: volume
methods.summary: The volume method is used to compute the volume of this instance if and only if this instance is manifold.
methods.remarks: The volume method is used to compute the volume of this instance if and only if this instance is manifold.

returns.doc: If the instance represents a manifold volume, volume will be a positive value. If volume is negative, the instance is not manifold.

properties.name: definition
properties.summary: The definition method is used to retrieve the component definition for this component instance.
properties.remarks: The definition method is used to retrieve the component definition for this component instance.

properties.name: glued_to
properties.summary: The #glued_to method is used to retrieve the entity that this instance is glued to.
properties.remarks: The #glued_to method is used to retrieve the entity that this instance is glued to.

properties.name: locked
properties.summary: The locked= method is used to lock a component instance.
properties.remarks: The locked= method is used to lock a component instance.

properties.name: name
properties.summary: The name method is used to get the name of this instance.
properties.remarks: The name method is used to get the name of this instance.

properties.name: transformation
properties.summary: The transformation method is used to retrieve the transformation of this instance.
properties.remarks: The transformation method is used to retrieve the transformation of this instance.
